subject: xone: fixed rumble.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Rumble timer delay calculations use uninitialized jiffies value, potentially causing incorrect rumble timing on first activation

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: input.cpp:rumble_input_device() uses EVIOCRMFF ioctl and ff_effect.u.rumble

license_provenance: GPL-2.0-or-later (vendored medusalix/xone; now sourced from dlundqvist/xone which carries same license)
notes:
This commit was part of MiSTer's vendored xone driver (medusalix/xone base). MiSTer's kernel no longer vendors xone—it's now a Buildroot package sourced from dlundqvist/xone (P3.2, re-sourced deliberate). The dlundqvist fork contains functionally equivalent rumble handling: it initializes rumble->last=jiffies in the gip_gamepad_send_rumble function (line 167) which is called during gip_gamepad_init_rumble initialization, achieving the same effect as this commit's upfront initialization but via a different code path. xone.mk:42-49 explicitly confirms: 'All FOUR of MiSTer's local deltas turned out to be unnecessary as separate patches...driver/gamepad.c already carries rumble handling'. Disposition is dropped-deliberate because: (1) xone is no longer vendored, (2) the packaged dlundqvist/xone includes equivalent functionality, (3) no separate patch needed. Silent-regression risk is low since dlundqvist's init-time send_rumble call sets the baseline correctly.

sonnet-verified: the 'line 167' supersession claim was RE-DERIVED against the authoritative local extract of the exact pinned source (Buildroot hash-verified: /mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/output/build/xone-f2aa9fe01103d7600553b505b298ff0bd47ff280/driver/gamepad.c) and CONFIRMED PRECISE: `gip_gamepad_send_rumble()` begins at line 151 and contains `rumble->last = jiffies;` at exactly line 167; `gip_gamepad_init_rumble()` (line 197) calls `gip_gamepad_send_rumble(&rumble->timer)` unconditionally at line 211 during device init (under the comment '/* stop rumble (required for some exotic gamepads to start input) */', line 205), so rumble->last IS set to jiffies at probe time before `gip_gamepad_queue_rumble()` (line 172) can ever read it at line 190's `mod_timer(&rumble->timer, rumble->last + GIP_GP_RUMBLE_DELAY)`. The claim of functional equivalence to this commit's upfront `gamepad->rumble.last = jiffies` (a one-line addition in gip_gamepad_init_input, confirmed via `git show 5a79654` in the fork repo) holds. Verification detour worth recording: an intermediate WebFetch of the same file from raw.githubusercontent.com returned FABRICATED function boundaries (claimed send_rumble at lines 133-147, init_rumble at 150-167, and that last was never initialized at probe) which briefly and wrongly 'contradicted' this record; the local hash-verified extract is definitive and vindicates the original citation. Disposition=dropped-deliberate and severity confirmed correct.
